mechanism_narrative: A higher FEDFUNDS level mechanically increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity present value and producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of always-on observations when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average, or Spearman rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS exceeding 1.5% signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending that precedes downward earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC is negative in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in INDPRO and HOUST indicate a simultaneous manufacturing and housing contraction that amplifies recession risk beyond either sector alone, generating negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and HOUST does not outperform the best single-series signal in predicting negative 20-day returns, or walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising unemployment, an unexpected month-over-month increase in PAYEMS indicates data volatility or measurement noise that resolves downward in subsequent revisions, causing temporary risk-off and negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In high-unemployment regimes, positive PAYEMS surprises are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, contradicting the negative prediction.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate, proxied by FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation, exceeds 1.5%, restrictive financial conditions dominate growth expectations and produce negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Episodes of high real rates do not coincide with negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Lower UNRATE signals tight labor markets that compress corporate profit margins via wage cost acceleration, generating negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative correlation between UNRATE level and 20-day forward returns breaks down (Rank IC > -0.02) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in HOUST above 5% signals a reacceleration in residential investment that drives cyclical earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is below its 12-month moving average, a concurrent decline in PAYEMS confirms a synchronized labor-manufacturing recession, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO below 12-month moving average and PAYEMS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or the interaction term adds no predictive power over INDPRO alone.
